metadata.description: Golden dataset for FastAPI RAG system evaluation

questions.question: How do I define path parameters in FastAPI?
questions.expected_answer: Use curly braces in the path and add them as function parameters, e.g., @app.get('/items/{item_id}') def read_item(item_id: int)

questions.question: What is the purpose of the @app.get() decorator?
questions.expected_answer: It defines an HTTP GET endpoint. The path specified in @app.get('/path') becomes the URL route for that endpoint.

questions.question: How do I enable CORS in FastAPI?
questions.expected_answer: Use CORSMiddleware from fastapi.middleware.cors and add it with app.add_middleware(). Configure allowed origins, methods, and headers.

questions.question: What is a Request Body in FastAPI?
questions.expected_answer: Data sent by the client in the body of a POST/PUT request, typically JSON. Define it using Pydantic models.

questions.question: How do I return a custom status code?
questions.expected_answer: Use the status_code parameter in the decorator, e.g., @app.post('/items', status_code=201) or return Response with status_code.

questions.question: What is Pydantic used for in FastAPI?
questions.expected_answer: Data validation and serialization. Define models that validate request/response data automatically.

questions.question: How do I define query parameters?
questions.expected_answer: Add them as function parameters not in the path. FastAPI automatically treats them as query params, e.g., def read_items(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 10)

questions.question: What is the difference between Query and Body parameters?
questions.expected_answer: Query parameters go in the URL (?key=value), Body parameters are sent in the request body (typically JSON).

questions.question: How do I add API documentation?
questions.expected_answer: FastAPI automatically generates docs at /docs (Swagger UI) and /redoc (ReDoc) based on your code.

questions.question: What is async def used for in FastAPI?
questions.expected_answer: Defines an asynchronous endpoint that can handle concurrent requests efficiently. Use for I/O-bound operations.

questions.question: How do I validate that a field is required?
questions.expected_answer: Don't provide a default value in the Pydantic model. Fields without defaults are required.

questions.question: How do I add a description to a query parameter?
questions.expected_answer: Use Query() with description parameter: item_id: int = Query(..., description='The ID of the item')

questions.question: What is dependency injection in FastAPI?
questions.expected_answer: A pattern where you define reusable dependencies (auth, DB connections, etc.) using Depends() that FastAPI executes automatically.

questions.question: How do I raise an HTTP exception?
questions.expected_answer: Use HTTPException from fastapi: raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail='Item not found')

questions.question: What is the purpose of response_model?
questions.expected_answer: Defines the Pydantic model for the response, enabling validation, serialization, and automatic docs generation.

questions.question: How do I read request headers?
questions.expected_answer: Use Header() parameter: user_agent: str = Header(None)

questions.question: How do I set response headers?
questions.expected_answer: Use Response object: return Response(content='...', headers={'X-Custom': 'value'})

questions.question: What is the difference between def and async def endpoints?
questions.expected_answer: async def uses asynchronous I/O (non-blocking), def runs in a thread pool (blocking). Use async for I/O-bound work.

questions.question: How do I upload files?
questions.expected_answer: Use File() and UploadFile: file: UploadFile = File(...). UploadFile provides async methods to read file content.

questions.question: How do I run background tasks?
questions.expected_answer: Use BackgroundTasks: add background_tasks.add_task(function, *args) to execute after returning response.

questions.question: How do I combine path, query, and body parameters in one endpoint?
questions.expected_answer: Define all in the function signature: path params in route, query params as function args with defaults, body params as Pydantic models.

questions.question: How do I validate that a string matches a regex pattern?
questions.expected_answer: Use Query() or Field() with regex parameter: name: str = Query(..., regex='^[a-z]+$')

questions.question: What's the difference between Form() and Body()?
questions.expected_answer: Form() expects application/x-www-form-urlencoded data (HTML forms), Body() expects application/json.

questions.question: How do I create nested Pydantic models?
questions.expected_answer: Define one model as a field type in another: class Image(BaseModel): url: str; class Item(BaseModel): image: Image

questions.question: How do I handle multiple response status codes?
questions.expected_answer: Use responses parameter in decorator with dict of status codes and models: @app.get('/items', responses={404: {'model': Error}})

questions.question: How do I implement authentication with OAuth2?
questions.expected_answer: Use OAuth2PasswordBearer for token dependency, create /token endpoint for login, verify tokens in dependencies.

questions.question: How do I organize routes in multiple files?
questions.expected_answer: Use APIRouter in separate files, then include them in main app with app.include_router(router, prefix='/api')

questions.question: How do I implement custom middleware?
questions.expected_answer: Create async function with app, call_next params. Use @app.middleware('http') decorator or app.add_middleware(). Process request before/after calling await call_next(request).

questions.question: How do I handle WebSocket connections?
questions.expected_answer: Use @app.websocket('/ws') decorator, accept connection with await websocket.accept(), then receive/send data in loop.

questions.question: How do I test FastAPI endpoints with pytest?
questions.expected_answer: Use TestClient from fastapi.testclient, create client = TestClient(app), then use client.get/post/etc. Returns standard responses for assertions.
